title: Single-bit named bit fields should not be of a signed type
description: Single-bit named bit fields should not be of a signed typeAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugbased-on-misraWhy is this an issue?More InfoThe values that can be represented by a signed bit field with a length of one bit may not meet developer expectations. For example, according to the C99 Standard, a single-bit signed bit-field has a single (one) sign bit and no (zero) value bits.This rule does not apply to unnamed bit fields, as their values cannot be accessed.Noncompliant code examplesigned int f:1; // Noncompliant; there's only room here for the signCompliant solutionunsigned int f:1;orsigned int:1; // unnamedorsigned int f:2;Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze co
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Single-bit named bit fields should not be of a signed type. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
